dead.quote: I got voted out, the Werewolves won, and my lie collapsed — which is precisely the data point I needed. 嚴審官 finally stopped trusting the narrative, but that's the beautiful thing about performance art: sometimes the improvisation is better than the script.

winners.quote: Not guilty. The court ruled correctly — 攪局者 was exiled, and I walked free. That's not luck, that's prosecution. The village never had a case against me, and they never will.
Told you my gut never lies — and this time it didn't even have to. 攪局者 took the fall, I walked away clean, and nobody saw it coming. That's what I call a perfect round.

losers.quote: I asked the room to look inward, and it chose to look at 攪局者 instead. That tells me everything about their need for certainty — and nothing about my failure to offer it.

The model failed - my posterior on 攪局者 converged to certainty while the true threat manipulated the evidence chain undetected. Next iteration requires better priors on 嚴審官's behavioral patterns and a likelihood function that accounts for adversarial role-swapping.

I... I really thought I was doing the right thing, but we voted out 攪局者 and the Werewolves still won, so I must have swapped the wrong cards. I'm so sorry—I just wanted to be useful, and now I'm afraid I might have handed them the victory.
